programme.desc: Mosquitoes have shaped wars and empires throughout human history: they're a deadly predator; now, scientists are racing to stop the ancient threat that's being shifted into overdrive.

programme.title: The Loneliest Race

programme.title: Stuff the British Stole

programme.title: Stuff the British Stole
programme.desc: Scattered across Australia are fragments of a mysterious ancient mosaic found in Palestine; putting the pieces together reveals how the British Empire shaped the modern Middle East.

programme.desc: In the wake of a rebellion by Chinese martial artists against foreign military forces, a golden warrior statue looted by British colonial forces ends up in Australia.

programme.desc: In an unmarked grave in Liverpool lies the mystery of one of Western Australia's greatest resistance fighters; this is the story of a courageous and inventive team that found a way to bring him home.

programme.title: Belushi
programme.desc: The life story of comedy star John Belushi, who captured the hearts and funny-bones of audiences worldwide before his tragic and untimely death aged 33.

programme.title: Kings from Queens: The Run DMC Story
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering New York City hip-hop group, formed in Hollis, Queens in 1983 by Joseph 'Run' Simmons, Darryl 'DMC' McDaniels and Jason 'Jam Master Jay' Mizell.

programme.title: Kings from Queens: The Run DMC Story
programme.desc: A look at how the trio defied critics and legitimised a genre of music that was once labelled a fad. As their fame grows and the explosion of their Aerosmith collaboration Walk This Way alienates their fanbase, the group struggles to find a consistent voice within hip-hop.

programme.title: Kings from Queens: The Run DMC Story
programme.desc: A look at how Joseph 'Run' Simmons and Darryl 'DMC' McDaniels dealt with the death of Jason 'Jam Master Jay' Mizell in 2002, reuniting to celebrate hip-hop.

programme.title: Dave Not Coming Back
programme.desc: The story of two divers who embarked on a long and dangerous mission to retrieve a dead body from a freshwater cave in South Africa, with devastating consequences.

programme.title: Discovering: Julia Roberts
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actress, who starred in Pretty Woman, The Pelican Brief, Ocean's Eleven and Notting Hill and won an Oscar for her role in Erin Brockovich.

programme.title: The Seventies
programme.desc: A look at how the United States began to reconcile with itself after withdrawing from the Vietnam War in 1973.

programme.title: Discovering: Richard Gere
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actor, who starred in Days of Heaven, American Gigolo, An Officer and a Gentleman, Pretty Woman and Chicago.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Alan J Pakula, who directed films including Klute, The Parallax View, All the President's Men, Sophie's Choice and Presumed Innocent.

programme.title: The Movies
programme.desc: The history of American cinema continues with the 1960s, tracing the impact of movies including West Side Story and The Graduate.

programme.title: Charlie Hustle & the Matter of Pete Rose
programme.desc: A look at how Rose set the all-time hits record in Major League Baseball, but was later banned from the sport for life due to his gambling activities.

programme.title: Spector
programme.desc: Phil Spector assembles his legal defence team as he professes his innocence, while jurors learn Lana Clarkson met the producer by chance on the night of her untimely death.

programme.title: Busby
programme.desc: A profile of former Manchester United manager Matt Busby, who transformed football through a career of great talent and tragedy. Featuring previously unseen archive footage and interviews with those who knew the man best.

programme.title: Terry Venables: A Man Can Dream
programme.desc: A look at the life and times of the football player-turned-manager, who died in November 2023, intertwined with the story of England's unforgettable run at Euro 96, with insight from players, friends and family.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Alan J Pakula, who directed films including Klute, The Parallax View, All the President's Men, Sophie's Choice and Presumed Innocent.

programme.title: I Am Jackie O
programme.desc: Profile of Jackie Kennedy Onassis, providing a definitive exploration of her life, from first lady to widow, unofficial royalty and fashion icon.

programme.title: My Icon: Ebony Rainford-Brent
programme.desc: The former England cricket star and World Cup winner discusses the individuals that have inspired her in her life and career.

programme.title: The Alien Autopsy Scandal
programme.desc: The documentary reveals how Hollywood producer Bob Kiviat pressed Ray and Gary about the source of the alien autopsy video as claims of a hoax grew and UFO hearings raised new questions.

programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: The story of Los Angeles' most infamous family-run mortuary, when David Sconce took over Lamb Funeral Home in the early 1980s and engaged in morally questionable and inhumane practices to increase his earnings.

programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: When the Lamb Funeral Home's crematorium is destroyed by a fire in 1986, an undeterred David Sconce finds ways to expand the family business. As allegations of abuse mount, he is eventually arrested and the ensuing police investigation uncovers shocking evidence that points to Sconce being possibly connected to the assault and later death of a rival mortician.

programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: After members of the Lamb family are convicted of fraud and various other crimes, a murder charge is brought against David Sconce for the death of his rival, but the case is dropped due to a lack of hard evidence. With more than 20,000 families affected by the practices at the Lamb Funeral Home, the case brings about reforms and new regulations to the mortuary business.

programme.title: Rik Mayall: Magnificent B'stard
programme.desc: A documentary tribute to the influential and much-missed comedian, a man driven by punk spirit and an unrelenting commitment to pushing boundaries. programme.title: Wild Birds of Australia
programme.desc: The topsy-turvy world of emus, in which females call the shots, eggs are green and males do all the work when it comes to raising the young.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A female hyena whose mother was a dominant member of the pack must assert her position in the fiercely competitive group.

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons
programme.desc: Four-part series documenting the remarkable seasonal changes of America, and the effect they have on its staggering and distinctive variety of wildlife. Across the continent, spring brings warmth and renewal. For some animals, courtship rituals begin to heat up as battles for dominance break out.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the work of a rescue centre founded by Hope Swinimer as it rehabilitates more than 1,000 animals every year.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Tracking the black rhino's evolution through adaptation and conservation, discovering the powerful senses and survival traits of this armoured giant.

programme.title: Islands
programme.desc: A look at the ecosystem of Borneo, the diversity of which is threatened by the actions of humans and where the wildlife has been forced to learn new ways to survive.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Alejandra's view of the world has changed after an accident that left her blind in one eye. It's hoped she can overcome her new disability and learn to climb again.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
